reviews.review_description:
Love the game. Each guy has a different style I just wish there were more guys to fight. But over all it's the second best boxing game on vr rn second to boxing underdog. Only second bc there's just not enough to it

Secret tip... After you win a fight knock your opponent out again and once he gets back up he'll continue fighting so you can go however many rounds you want without having to restart matches

I downloaded this game after sinking lord knows how many hours into thrill of the fight. It has great graphics, the ai can be very smart and the punches are satisfying. The problems are it seems very early access, there are only 3 men to fight, zero customisation on the rounds, attributes of opponents or time. It has a training section coming and hopefully some more fighters and tweaks, for example, the second fighter sometimes gets his own arm stuck behind his back. Overall, I really hope this game isn't abandoned because I'd love to see it flourish more, it has the potential to be the best vr boxing game.

Early access, so yh it's not complete, but still there's no settings for the length of fights, only 3 opponents (which are good, the ais solid) but they all seem slightly too big to me. You won't win easily generally it's very reactive

This game has some good parts and some bad parts. The good part is that the ai moves very legit each fighter has their own style and can suprise you also wiith each hit the controller vibrates which makes it very satisfying. Apart from that its a bit mid its just not that outstanding or special, you can only fight 3 fighters and all the animation are not that special in contrast with creed for example. Also the gameplay and boxing is mid the ai often move back and when i try to move forward i get out the screen and cant see anything but you also get knocked out way too easily i have to hit the bot for like 10 times on the head and hes still standing while when i get hit once i already see black. If you like a realistic movement from ai and just a simple game for a workout once in a while this game is good but if you want to play this more often i wouldnt recommend

Honestly I kinda wanted to give this a 2 star, but there is enough innovative stuff here that it didn't feel quite right to do that. I bought this game at 4:15 today and at aprox. 4:42 I had beaten the whole thing. Each guy at every level. This is a game probably worth about 10 bucks but I understand that honestly there is profit threshold these guys need to operate with when developing a game so I don't mind the extra 5. Everyone here has already mentioned the ai stepping out of the play area, the goofy ragdolling, the immense livershot weakness, and few other bits. 

On the plus side- The ai isn't as formulaic as other titles and doesn't rely on the same patterns to throw punches. After a bit you can predict whats gonna come next but to be fair that's pretty normal, if you throw a right hook you can bet your butt his left is coming. I like that body shots matter, even if it is a bit goofy. The ai reacts to feints, which is cool. Hand tracking doesn't always align well but ...it wouldn't be a boxing vr game if your glove didn't randomly decide desync  from time to time. Jabs are worth nothing in this game as with all boxing games (except CRtG) so just remember to throw hard-fast-and don't bother to set it up, you'll only warn them what's coming. 

All in all? Meh. It's ok.
Does not let you move by the controlr im 5. 3 so i cant get him

reviews.review_title: Games been updated and it’s amazing.
reviews.review_description: Forget the negative reviews from the past, we’re gonna talk about right now. Games graphics been changed to a more realistic, polished look and it’s great, punches are cleaner and bugs have been for sure deleted as the gameplay is clear as day, bruises and damage are looking good and the new character is also very challenging and hard to beat. I like the ragdolls and how fun it is for you to be able to punch them on the ground, overall the game is a 9/10 and it’ll will get better in the future as the developers are back to working on this game.

Love this game but still a work in progress.  The AI opponents are very realistic and have some of the best movement in VR boxing.  Now that the developer is back updating, I am excited for this game to reach its potential.  If it was a finished product, I'd give it 5 stars.

I have been playing Thrill of the Fight 1.0 since its release many years ago. The single player has been a major part of my workout. Unfortunately the new one went the multiplayer route and completely forgot about single players. So I've been trying to find something more modern that can get the job done.

The last update of Ultra changes everything. It has now replaced TOTF in my workout. The fighters don't just move back and forth, but in all directions. The roll and move around. This adds a whole new dimension. The graphics are a huge improvement over TOTF as well.

Another game changer is that Ultra remembers your fights and you can compare your performance as you go. I was able to measure the difference in my punches wearing a Cobra Strap and having my arms free. I found that amazing and worth the measly cost of the simulation.

It supports MR which is a must at this point. No one wants to punch a wall. Unfortunately, as of this writing, the fighters are not aware of where my walls and furniture are and sometimes they go through the wall when you are trying to get them against it for pounding. It would be great to have a fight all around your place.

The only other drawback is that you can't set round times yet, which seems like a small oversite that could be fixed in a day and I'm sure will be before final release.

Unlike TOTF 1, you can't control various settings of your opponents, but you do have the ability to turn off knock downs as well as knockouts and that's HUGE. If you like experimenting and don't want your opponent to go down or maybe yourself, it's a simple click.

I would love to see hand tracking added. My problem with all VR boxing games is that you are not holding your fist correctly because you have a controller in it.

Anyways, this simulation is easily worth the price. Check it out.

If this update had dropped six months ago, I’d feel really optimistic about where the game is heading. But after over a year, I expected a lot more. There are definitely some great improvements, but this still feels like a half-step.

The Good:
This update solidifies this as the most realistic boxing sim out there. The graphics are noticeably better, movement is more fluid, and the AI now behaves like actual boxers. They react to feints, go defensive after eating shots—it’s awesome. That part of the game feels alive now.

The Bad:
Nothing truly new was added. We got one new fighter, but there’s still no training mode, no career mode, and no meaningful customization. A major bug where the AI fights outside your boundary is still there, which is crazy after all this time.

This is a good update. It genuinely improves the game. But after a year, it just doesn’t feel like enough.

What Gives Me Hope:
The best thing about this update is that it shows the devs get it. Their focus is clearly on the boxing itself—not fluff like shoe colors or hairstyles (looking at you, TOTF 2). That gives me confidence in the long-term vision.

But here’s the thing: you guys are on the brink of something huge. With TOTF 2 flopping, you now have the chance to take over the entire VR boxing space. But to do that, you have to move faster. Spend the money. Hire the talent. Don’t sit on this momentum.

You’ve already built the best boxing engine—now finish the game.

I have most of the boxing games available for Quest. 
This one absolutely hits 👌 , it is actually motivating me to train hard, 
Visuals are just perfect!  I play in MR mostly and so great to see motivating, challenging opponents in my living room, AI are in a different league compared to everything available today,  way way ahead! .
Obviously, this is still an early access of the game, yet these devs set the expectations in the right direction. 
I am glad this game is live and kicking.
And as i said it is kicking hard.
This is the game to grow with... if you novice or more experienced.

Very nice, well done developers! finally the framerate problems in the animation have disappeared, now it is much more fluid, the movements of the opponents are definitely satisfying. The graphics of the opponents have evolved towards greater realism. the dynamic shadows are excellent. 

the feedback of the combat is very good, the effects of the blows on the face are ok, both when you give them and when you receive them, only that I am not entirely sure how the enemy dissolves into semi-transparency, why did you choose this effect? ​​
maybe it would be better if the enemy becomes blurred for a moment? (but I understand that it would be more demanding to process as an effect for the processor) so would it be fine just to darken as it already is but without the enemy becoming semi-transparent?

I'm not entirely happy with the effect of punches on the stomach or sides, I hardly ever punch in that area and when I do I don't get much feedback, but it's still ok since you mainly punch the face.

when you hit the enemy the sweat droplets that splash in the air are not displayed in the right place but a little further back and moved to the right.

playing in passthrough is definitely the best choice, and I wanted to ask if you plan to introduce static occlusion too, to have another game mode where the opponent respects the collisions of walls and real objects and moves based on the available space in the room, this is more difficult to achieve but it would be great from a realism point of view.

And I also imagine dynamic shadows could adapt to the surface of furniture and walls in this mode, which would make it even more realistic.

Alternatively, you could implement a feature where, if the Meta-scanned room is available, the opponent—while not respecting collisions with real objects and walls—becomes semi-transparent exactly at the points where they intersect or pass through real objects. This way, it would give the impression that the opponent is, for example, inside a wardrobe. This would also prevent more instinctive players, caught up in the heat of the fight, from accidentally punching their own furniture, as they would see the opponent through the object but wouldn’t realize they are hitting the real furniture. This could be an optional feature enabled by default (if the scanned room is present and available) or disabled by the player.

anyway thanks for this great and unexpected update 👍👍👍🤙👋
Very realistic opponents! Very fun and good workout!

reviews.review_title: Most natural boxing game out there
reviews.review_description: I play all the boxing games.  With the final release of Jamal - it really makes this game more of a challenge.  I love the way the players move.  The latest release seems to have made the first character harder which is what I want.  The newest character, Jamal made me work through all five rounds - This is what I was looking for.  Like the previous review before me 9/10.  Good job and keep up the good work.  My only criticism - have the players do a slightly better job blocking their bodies.  But just a smidge lol.

$hitti$t boxing game I’ve played including random $5 steam games they do to much in the areas that don’t matter or hinder your playing experience and it just ruins it when I watch the same hook I’ve been throwing 2/5 times just slap their face like a flaccid sausage and then they hit me from 6 feet away but miss a jab when I’m not blocking at all can’t say how sad this game makes me after playing

reviews.review_title: Still far from being any good
reviews.review_description:
When it came out it was just released as early access so I gave it 4 stars out of 5 and said "Promising. let's give it time".
I am forced to bring it down to 2.

NOTE!!!!: I AM A DISASTER IN FIGHTING SPORTS, I am just an heavy powerlifter who has just hopped a few days in some combat sports for fun now and then. Any junior boxer would knock me down after a few months of training.

Now, many many many months lather and many updates later, I managed to beat at normal difficulty all the boxers at first round up to jamal (the only one I won by points and not by TKO).

The AI dodging happens only on the face and Jamal is the only one that does cover his body a little.
You just make some jabs and as soon as they get to dodge you fire  some missile uppercut to the body and here they go down.

Just like that. I Literally flying-ragdolled them to the ringside with some lateral body hook, it looked like some comical cartoon.

If the game is meant to refine your skills in trying to hit someone on the head then it's another story, but as it is now I can just beat everyone quick and from what I saw increasing difficulty only means they are more resistant and attack more. Which won't save them from unleashing a full power uppercut to the body

Honestly, its a good game, the physics, AI and gameplay. But the only bad thing is when i have this game downloaded whenever i play Steam Link to play Bone lab or anything, it randomly crashes. I don’t know if its my problem or the devs. Im sure an update would fix that issue. Other than that great game, if i wanted to play it again, i would have to redownload it then uninstall it again.

Not sure about the previous reviewer. I haven't played the game for a few months waiting for Jamal. Back in today and the game is now very janky and everything is off. The calibration is reading 20cm higher than I actually am (I'm only 160cm and it's reading 179cm) so all the fighters are now towering over me. The fighters look very cartoony now. Jamal just weaves alot...wow...that was worth the months of waiting and all the hype 🙄After fights the b button to exit doesn't work so you have to quit the game and go back in again. What happened to this game it used to be really good!

Opponents are great! Comparing to TOTF 1, it adds another dimension on defense and has a better graphics. (However TOTF 1&2 has more solid mechanics/realism in terms of response/feedback)

The impact system (punching contact) power feedback/response could be improved, currently the user's gloves goes through and through in the opponents body making their response look same regardless of the power. 

Nevertheless it's dope! Looking forward to any updates whenever possible! 🙏

The game is okay but the lack of opponents and how they don't stay in your boundary is really horrible I like playing in my room with a fairly decent amount of space but every time I would try and pressure they always end up in the same area which is very close to my TV but overall not a horrible game just needs more work

This game has great potential. Great to see the game has been updated. And great to see that the developer’s have followed up to confirm that the game will continue being developed. Already a great game with the most realistic ai movement of all the boxing games. Looking forward to further updates.

UPDATE: June 2025

After zero communication since last year, they shadow dropped the promised update.
Much improved graphics: Improved lighting, textures really sell the physical presence of someone standing in front of you in MR mode.
 (the best of al released VR boxing games so far) 
& finally the promised new character has been added

Hopefully there’s more content coming, but what they do have is fun & looks great. 
Fingers crossed there’s actually some communication this time.

One criticism is the AI doesn’t block body shots that well.     
  It’s bit too easy to just spam hooks to the opponents body for the majority of the fight. I’d like to see them drop their hands more as the body gets damaged    


UPDATE: March 2025
  


Devs promised an updated with another fight by December 2024, then just disappeared.

There’s been no communication on their discord since.
Game was promising but unfinished.

The game is very decent when it comes to the actual combat and damage, and the physics aren't too bad either. My only real complaint is how it's EXCLUSIVELY roomscale (some people don't have much room in their homes) and the lack of communication from the devs. They dropped a new fighter out of nowhere after a year with no update, or any updates on development for that matter. But aside from that, they game is very nice and I reccomend it.

I would say it's the worst boxing game I have played.  if you like buggy games and unrealistic movements this might be your game... if you can even call it that. Spent 15 bucks and the game ( not even joking ) took more time to load than it did to beat. So pissed and want my money back..   being charged for this game was less satisfying then being robbed... dont waste money,  don't worry not much time would be wasted though.

developer_response.body: Hey @worstsoldierever, since you mentioned wanting your money back, here are the steps to request a refund: 1) On your Meta Horizon Mobile App, open Menu 2) Select purchase history 3) Select the game and click three dots 4) Choose "request a refund"

reviews.review_description: Update: The recent Update made this game 10 times better the new character "Jaamal" he is more of a challenge and I like that so if your looking for a good boxing game to play with realistic knockouts I recommend this game right here

reviews.review_title: Boxing experience worth to pay for
reviews.review_description: I really like this game. Every oponent is distinct and interesting to fight. Graphics look very good on quest 2. Issues with punches registering were resolved in the recent update. Now the gameplay is smooth and entertaining.

Now three fighters available

Even with updates the game is still very easy to beat.

Glove positioning and hit detection have been improved over time but still need extensive work.

Ai is fun with a bit of character and flash to the movements but nothing that made it feel more enhanced as it claims. Just seems to have different motion capture for the boxers. Id say AI claims are very misleading at this stage

Combat is decent with lots of potential and I'm sure would only get better... if they didnt abandon game... now id warn people that this game likely will never feel fully polished. So if would you buy the game in its current state for 3 sub par fighters ?
This game is pretty realistic ngl it reminds me of boxing underdog and this is the closest thing we have to that. Overall great game I wish I could put more stars :]

reviews.review_title: A bit bad and a bit good
reviews.review_description:
While playing my opponent kept backing up and I kept almost hitting my TV or a table. Other than that I was able to hit them a lot Anf it had passthrpugh so I could know if I needed to back up
(I MEAN THAT IT WAS GOOD BECAUSE IT HAD PASSTHROUGH JUST SO YALL KNOW)

developer_response.body:
Hey @MetaQuestAks3lllll, the game actually has passthrough, you can enable it after you open the game just below the "Fight" button.
If you need help, join our Discord https://ultraboxingvr.com/discord/

reviews.review_description:
This game has so much potential and the game is really good so far, it has a few bugs but other that the game is really good

The ai is amazing

The gameplay is really good.

reviews.review_title: I was wrong about my initial review
reviews.review_description: Very well made devs are actually actively working on polishing and the ai w dev team and game
Great app. The fighters actually move like real boxers. Great workout.
Nice update!! I like the new graphic look!
The physics behind it are just insane
Still abandoned.
THEY UPDATED IT ITS SO GOOD

The developers have really worked a miracle. After this update UltraBoxing VR feels like a completely new game. Graphics became much more realistic - lighting, shadows, fighters' skin look just gorgeous. The new opponent Jamaal is a special treat: he fights competently, aggressively, defends himself like a live person, and in general creates the feeling of a real fight. Animations, MOCAP, sounds - everything became denser, more powerful and atmospheric.

You're not just waving your arms, you're boxing. The game now looks and feels like a real simulator - seriously, the level has grown by leaps and bounds.

But there is one minus - after the update, the game stopped seeing the boundaries of the room (guardian) correctly. This is a nuisance, especially in intense battles - you can easily go out of bounds and crash into something. I hope it will be fixed in the next patches.

Otherwise, thanks a lot to the team. This is a real next level 👊🔥
They have been teasing a new character since November that was going to be out before Christmas. It’s now mid march with no update. Save your money and buy TOTF instead.

developer_response.body: Hey @Zeus.987! Yes, the update took much longer than expected, but it's here! We also have many more planned. Check it out and leave your feedback here or in our Discord community: https://ultraboxingvr.com/discord

reviews.review_description: This game is really fun for me when I just want to put on my VR headset and blow off some steam it is very realistic the AI is very smart the game tracks how hard you punch so it is accurate whenever the AI is knocked out I am in contact with one of the developers and I know that this game is going to go somewhere great game you should get it
I can’t play and it won’t load and it’s super laggy

developer_response.body:
Hi credstuxx,
Sorry you're experiencing these issues! We just released an update. Please try the latest version and let us know if you're still having trouble in our Discord.

reviews.review_title: A Good Start - Unsure About Development
reviews.review_description:
First thing first, what the developers have accomplished in terms of smooth and relatively smart AI, wow. I have played all of the most popular vr boxing games on the Quest 2 (Thrill of the Fight 1 & 2, Golden Gloves, Creed, Crazy Kung Fu) and I was blown away by the fighters and they way it looks. I thought, this is what modern-vr that is not 'realistic graphics' should be.

I am a fan of the way damage is telegraphed on the npc fighter. This is why I gave 3 Stars.

The game is, in my opinion, is Alpha or Beta state - barely early access, and it shows.

The discord shows a lot of interest, but feels like speaking into the wind (in terms of Dev interaction). 

I consider the money I paid, a contribution to the development of a potentially Championship-Level VR Boxing Game.

Overall verdict, if you're looking long term, Thrill of the Fight 1 or 2.

If you're interested in checking out potential next-level vr boxing and want to donate to the game developers. Give this a try.

developer_response.body: Hey DigitalByooki! The game is in active development. We've released an update recently. Check it out and share your feedback!

reviews.review_title: Most realistic boxing game out there
reviews.review_description:
I'm a boxer and this is the closest to real fighting/sparring that you can get. Amazing what has been achieved here.

Does anybody have any further news on development?

Punches goes through the NPC’s, when it connects doesnt provide any sensation. 2 characters to fight and nothing else for 15 bucks. The devs won’t give this 3.9 stars. I didn’t get whats up with all the hype…
Punching at the gloves arms legs doesn't do anything a jab doesn't do anything it's not calibrated correctly punches are way off. It's now February 2025 and no new fighter update.................

developer_response.body: We recently released a new update with a new fighter and other improvements. We'd love for you to check it out and share your feedback either here or in our Discord community https://ultraboxingvr.com/discord

reviews.review_description: I have Creed and The Main Event and this one is the closest to actual real boxing yet. The other 2 have some predictable tells in them that make it easier to win but this one doesn’t seem to do that, it seems to know your moves. It counters and is offensive. Can’t wait for the full version to be released.
This games is good to help your kids to practice boxing this is like real boxing but just in a games 4 stars🥇👍

reviews.review_title: Worst VR Boxing Game I've played!
reviews.review_description:
Coming from TOTF, I was under the impression that this would offer a more realistic experience since I am constantly bombarded by their ads on reddit.

I WAS WRONG. The game runs slow and my punches barely registers. The low FPS makes this game unplayable for me.

I'm still hoping this game would improve in the future but right now it just doesn't have the right to be priced higher than TOTF when this game is barely playable.

developer_response.body: Hey xtiandvd! Check out our recent update – we've added a new fighter and made many improvements. We'd love for you to share your feedback here or in our Discord community: https://ultraboxingvr.com/discord

reviews.review_description: This game was realistic.  I loved the physics.  You can experience real boxing without tasting blood in your mouth!

reviews.review_title: The most realistic boxing game in VR
reviews.review_description:
Congratulations on the game. Keep working hard and you will have a spectacular boxing game.
It would be cool if they included texts in Spanish in the game in the future. Also, to give more realism and to avoid repeating the NPCs, we could all participate and show our faces to the public. I would also add a ring entry sequence for the boxers, greater variety of Ring and that during the breaks we can see our opponent and us in the corner with our coach. By the way, the style of music you use in Jamal's trailer would be spectacular for the game menu.

I NEED A RUFUND NOW IT IS BAD
￼

reviews.review_title: The best boxing game for the price
reviews.review_description: Well i bought this because i got the meta quest 3s for my birthday and the game is good for still being in beta I’m glad there adding more things like the new person to fight and the training is coming soon

As of 2024 this game sucks! There is only 2 people you can fight in this game and thats the only thing you can do. something is wrong with the hit system i cant hit where i want to and my opponent just goes through my boundary any time i land a good combo he just simply backs up to where i can no longer hit him.

Overall my hopes were high for this game and i hate it im really hoping that the full game fixes it to your boundary and that i can throw more than 2 punches without my hands disappearing
Such a good game. If only this was multi-player.

By far the closest i’ve ever felt to actual sparring in VR - the pace/movement/feel is definitely there. Feints being registered/reacted to is an insane game changer. The detection feels a lot better with this update though still not perfect and it’s missing a sense of impact (could be the sound effects that make it feel like i’m hitting a bouncy wall of rubber). But the feel is really there. I’ve bought/played every VR boxing experience out there (literally every single one) and none captures the sport like this one.

If you train IRL, you have to check this one out. Even if you’ve only played ToTF, the pace/feel of this one shines - being able to pick your shots as you would in real life is an incredibly feeling, and there is no doubt massive improvements coming down the road. Really stoked to see how this development continues.

reviews.review_title: Good game dont get if not enough space
reviews.review_description: Accidentally smacked my controller into the wall and it snapped and broke dont get if you have less then 2m squared space

As others have stated, by far the closest to actual sparring you can currently get in a VR experience. While there are only 2 AI opponents at the moment (with more on the way), they both have very unique fighting  styles. The developer seems very keen on  developing the AI and movement system so that every opponent is unique - it's not simply a case of increased 'health' or 'speed' etc.

Strap some ankle and wrist weights on and this becomes a decent cardio workout - possibly better than many of the so-called fitness apps available.

While light on features due to its early access status, it's already well worth the price. Honestly it's light-years ahead of the other boxing games I've tried.

Can't wait to see this develop even further.

reviews.review_title: It gave me a pretty good workout!
reviews.review_description: If you like to exercise in VR, then I think you're going to like it :)

reviews.review_title: Doesn’t feel right, stick to ToTF!
reviews.review_description:
I want to start of by saying I’m comparing this game directly to ToTF and it doesn’t even come close.

Yes the opponents have better movement but what good is that movement when your hits don’t register. Now, on the rare chance the punches land where you intended, they don’t feel right. In ToTF, when I punch an opponent there is a sense of satisfaction that I can’t seem to explain, I don’t know if it’s controller feedback or sound feedback but it “feels” like I made contact, this game lacks that. 

I hope more updates come to this game because right now I feel like I wasted my money. I went back to playing ToTF the very next day.
Everything about the game was the worst. The feeling was horrible and you needed a lot of room, which I do not have in the slightest. Refunding. Not worth it.
When you fight a bot it is hard and the knockdown feels REAL it’s so fun soooooo recommend
Nothing to say it’s just bad

I’ve been boxing over a year and this game is just a cash grab totf1 is cheaper and miles better straight punches do nothing they leave your boundary and the game isn’t optimized on top of all of this meta won’t give me a refund

The AI responds quite well when the players engage, and the AI enemy will do counter attacks when players keep punching non-stop. Such interactions will give a good interactive sparring, and not just a dull AI sandbag. I enjoy this game so much and hope this game keep updating.
Not even polished or a finished game. Not even sure how this is being sold on meta.

Definitely one of the best boxing games Available in VR. The player animations are excellent and definitely add to the experience

this is 100% worth picking up and will be a pretty serious totf contender as the devs make theyre way to completion.

So as someone who has played creed and totf, 100-200+ hours of totf mainly , this game is amazing !!!! The crown jewel of this game is the AI movement, bobbing , weaving, amazing defensive and offensive movement. As someone who use to play totf everyday and got burnt out trying to to beat money maker on outclassed lol , this game was what I needed .  Some criticisms of the hit detection outside of the play area didn't bother me , you just  had to back up to get your opponent back in the play area , which I liked because it forced me to move and think about my footwork , that being said I would play in pass through mode , because your opponent goes out enough that I would have punched my wall if not lol . I burned 300 calories in 30mins , so 600 calories a hour , and this was me just playing the game not trying to go hard or anything . Really enjoyed the 15 second brakes , forced you right back into the fight . loved after you knocked opponent out , they got up after 6's ready to fight no messing around . Which brings me to the only thing I'd add to this game , a iron chin setting or a iron chinned opponent, basically something so you couldn't knock out your opponent. I've probably played way to many hours of vr boxing , the amazing movement and defensive skills of the opponents threw me off , but I was able to knock them out pretty easily . I'd just like a opponent I couldn't knock out , so I could test my boxing skills to see if I could win a decision on the judges score cards . To the developers , your game is amazing ,  u don't need multiplayer till way later , u don't need amazing environments or crowds , you already have the best AI movement in VR boxing , double down on that , add some more opponents, add some different difficulty levels and you will have the best VR Boxing game out there ! Sorry about the long post , I'm just really passionate about vr boxing and this game has the building blocks to be the #1 VR Boxing game , best of luck hope this game goes to the moon !

reviews.review_title: It’s really good for workout and fun.
reviews.review_description: I love it it’s like totf it’s so good and im a real life Muay Thai fighter so it’s easy for me but after I was done playing after like 20 mins I was drenched in water, so u should get it.
This game has a realistic AI and it's updated periodically. If the dec respects his promises, this will be the best boxing VR simulation

WOW! This is a very realistic boxing simulation. The AI movement is very close to what a real life opponent might be like as it bobs and weeves very close to what a real opponent might do. However do not be mistaken and think this is as fun or as good as the "thrill of the fight ". Yes it is a good game in its own right but the punches in this are somehow not as satisfying as the aforementioned game. My advice to the developer(s) would be to focus on the AI and not on the multiplayer until later... much later. I didn't do much boxing practice in my life but from what I do remember this game allows you to fully extend when you punch unlike the other game where you could just go in with a fury of punches. I like the fact that when fighting your opponent you have to properly focus on the shoulders and react accordingly. Would highly recommend this game to anyone who is looking for a challenge and is interested in fitness.

Wow! 

I love this game more than I do Thrill of the Fight 👀

Feels so much more realistic, it’s also amazingly challenging I’d so jump in a grab this now while it’s in Early Access before the price goes through the roof!! 

The UltraSense AI will BLOW YOUR MIND!!! The other fighter actually dodges your real time punches and defends themselves according to your attacks it’s absolutely unbelievable! 

For a game in Early Access wow your getting a lot for your cash! 

Well done devs amazing job! 🙌
Fits go straight through the ai not good game

developer_response.body: Hey @Carroteater16, check out the latest update – players are saying it's much better now!

reviews.review_description: After playing this game, I came to realize that it’s a lot better than thrill of the fight, but the enemies don’t ever throw body shots. A lot tougher the  thrill of the fight, the ai really bops and weaves. ￼￼
if you play on a quest it’s super slow and laggy waste of money creed is better than this game ong
Recommend this app to everyone who likes realistic boxing and fitness apps

reviews.review_title: Can't wait to see more updates!
reviews.review_description: I recently tried the early access version of Ultra Boxing on my Meta Quest, and it’s already shaping up to be one of the best VR boxing games out there. The realistic mechanics and intense workouts make it a fantastic choice for VR fitness enthusiasts. I love how the game reacts to my punches, offering a real challenge compared to other VR boxing games like Thrill of the Fight and Creed. The graphics are impressive, and the haptics feel just right, adding to the immersive experience. Although it's still in early development with limited fighters, the potential is clear. Can't wait to see more updates!

This early access game is already arguably better than The Thrill of The Fight, and with some funding and effort from the developers this could be the best VR boxing game period. Please, please, please, developers if you're reading this, don't drop this darn game, make it the game I truly know in my heart it could become.

Shotty hit detection and physics with a pretty low frame rate, but everything else is a straight upgrade from The Thrill of The Fight. 

If you enjoyed TOTF, certainly pick up this game.

Interface is still a bit wonkey, but still amazing game play, and very nice stats.  If I could choose the next thing to get added, it would be the ability to calibrate the gloves.  It's hard to find a comfortable grip.

Just wanna say that I’ve been playing thrill of the Fight all the time for about 3-4 years now, I was itching for something new and I’m very happy to have found this game via a post on Reddit. I am having a great time working out with this game so far. 

I am using the oculus quest 2 and I’d say: 

Graphics are looking better than TOTF👍🏾

The hits need to be more accurate, I felt like I got punched through my gloves and some spot on punches kinda miss.

The hit/Punching sounds effects sound like bouncy balls or balloons or some sort, I really hope that gets fixed.

I know it’s still in developement so I support💪🏾 I can’t wait to get full workouts with 12 round match options and more.

Extra Ideas that no one asked for😁:
YouTube added in the gym training room. 
Multiplayer in the future.
Customization.

reviews.review_title: The Ai's are better than TTOTF.
reviews.review_description: The AI's are much better and more realistic than the AI's you fight in The Thrill Of The Fight. Enough said. The game is still in development, so they're missing everything else that The Thrill Of The Fight has, but the AI's moving, dodging, and counter punching make it totally worth it. Please continue developing this game. Include everything The Thrill Of The Fight has, and it will automatically be a 10 out of 10.

The AI definitely is more responsive than other titles but an annoying tendency I found is they often backup beyond the boundary making it risky to follow them. Also I don't believe you can score a knockdown with a body shot, at least in my experience I never have ..  (would like some more feedback on this!) Thanks for the workout! Cheers!
